title: Dungeons & Dragons History
description: From Chainmail roots to modern editions, test your knowledge of the history of Dungeons & Dragons across 30 questions.

questions.text: In what year was the original Dungeons & Dragons boxed set first published?
questions.explanation: The original Dungeons & Dragons boxed set was first published in 1974 by Tactical Studies Rules (TSR).

questions.text: Who are the two primary co-creators of Dungeons & Dragons?
questions.explanation: D&D is primarily credited to Dave Arneson and Gary Gygax, who built on miniature wargaming concepts.

questions.text: What tabletop game directly influenced early D&D combat rules?
questions.explanation: Chainmail, a medieval miniatures wargame by Gygax and Jeff Perren, strongly influenced early D&D combat.

questions.text: What does TSR stand for?
questions.explanation: TSR stands for Tactical Studies Rules, the company that originally published D&D.

questions.text: What was the title commonly used for the original 1974 version of D&D?
questions.explanation: Fans commonly refer to the 1974 game as Original Dungeons & Dragons, or OD&D.

questions.text: Which class was NOT in the original 1974 rules?
questions.explanation: Paladin was introduced later; the original classes were Fighting-Man, Magic-User, and Cleric (with Thief added soon after).

questions.text: What is often considered the first published D&D adventure module?
questions.explanation: Palace of the Vampire Queen (1976), by Wee Warriors, is often cited as the first published D&D adventure module.

questions.text: When did Advanced Dungeons & Dragons (1st Edition) begin releasing core books?
questions.explanation: AD&D 1st Edition began with the Monster Manual in 1977, followed by the Player's Handbook (1978) and DMG (1979).

questions.text: Who wrote the AD&D 1st Edition Dungeon Masters Guide?
questions.explanation: Gary Gygax wrote the AD&D 1st Edition Dungeon Masters Guide, published in 1979.

questions.text: Which setting debuted in 1980 with Tracy and Laura Hickman module work and later novels?
questions.explanation: Dragonlance became a major D&D setting in the 1980s, tied closely to adventure modules and novels.

questions.text: What was the broad public panic around D&D in the 1980s often called?
questions.explanation: D&D was swept up in the broader Satanic Panic, despite no evidence linking the game to real-world harm.

questions.text: In 1983, which red-box Basic D&D set became especially famous?
questions.explanation: Frank Mentzer's 1983 Basic Set (the famous red box) was hugely influential for new players.

questions.text: What year did AD&D 2nd Edition release?
questions.explanation: AD&D 2nd Edition was released in 1989 and revised many rules and presentation choices from 1E.

questions.text: Which company acquired TSR in 1997?
questions.explanation: Wizards of the Coast acquired TSR in 1997, taking over publication of Dungeons & Dragons.

questions.text: Which edition introduced the d20 System and Open Game License era in 2000?
questions.explanation: D&D 3rd Edition launched in 2000 with the d20 System and OGL, significantly changing the RPG market.

questions.text: What is the common shorthand for the 2003 revision of 3rd Edition?
questions.explanation: The 2003 revision is commonly called D&D 3.5, updating and rebalancing many 3E rules.

questions.text: What year did D&D 4th Edition release?
questions.explanation: D&D 4th Edition released in 2008 with a strong tactical-combat focus and distinct class roles.

questions.text: Which game, released in 2009, was built from D&D 3.5 under the OGL and became a major competitor?
questions.explanation: Pathfinder (1st Edition) was built from the D&D 3.5 SRD under the OGL and became highly popular.

questions.text: What year did D&D 5th Edition first release?
questions.explanation: D&D 5th Edition launched in 2014 after a long public playtest period known as D&D Next.

questions.text: What was the public playtest name used before 5th Edition launched?
questions.explanation: Before 5E's release, Wizards ran an extensive public playtest under the name D&D Next.

questions.text: Which livestream and actual-play culture shift significantly boosted D&D's mainstream visibility in the 2010s?
questions.explanation: Actual-play streaming shows helped bring D&D to mainstream audiences and new generations of players.

questions.text: In 1999, which toy and game company acquired Wizards of the Coast?
questions.explanation: Hasbro acquired Wizards of the Coast in 1999, making D&D part of Hasbro's portfolio.

questions.text: Which classic world was Gary Gygax's original campaign setting?
questions.explanation: Greyhawk was Gary Gygax's foundational campaign setting and an early cornerstone of D&D lore.

questions.text: Who is the creator of the Forgotten Realms setting before it became official D&D material?
questions.explanation: Ed Greenwood created the Forgotten Realms, later adopted as a major official D&D setting.

questions.text: What famous 1978 module by Gary Gygax is known for deadly traps and a demilich?
questions.explanation: Tomb of Horrors is known for brutal traps and the demilich Acererak, and remains an iconic challenge module.

questions.text: Which mechanic remained a central identity of D&D across editions?
options: Rolling polyhedral dice, especially the d20
questions.explanation: Polyhedral dice, especially the d20 for core resolution in modern editions, are central to D&D's identity.

questions.text: What was the name of the open-content license used by Wizards during the 3E era?
questions.explanation: The Open Game License (OGL) allowed third-party publishing based on the System Reference Document.

questions.text: Which 5E adventure was released with and popularized the hardback campaign model in 2014?
questions.explanation: Hoard of the Dragon Queen was one of the first major 5E hardback campaigns and launched the Tyranny of Dragons storyline.

questions.text: In broad terms, how did 5E's design approach differ from 4E?
options: More focus on rulings and flexibility
questions.explanation: 5E intentionally emphasized simplicity, flexibility, and DM rulings compared to 4E's heavier tactical structure.

questions.text: What anniversary did D&D celebrate in 2024 based on its 1974 release year?
questions.explanation: From 1974 to 2024 marks 50 years of Dungeons & Dragons.
